commands_run: optcg-promote open-revision --lane reference (both bundles, entry at hypothesis)
optcg-reference-fit fit (JP bundle, two albedo strategies)

tests.detail: 202 python + 7 web tests green at holo-lab merge 3cdec96 before execution
manifest digests, media hashes, blocked-source acquisition tasks, tier consistency all valid

visual_checks.description: Exact-variant pixel verification of every acquired image (EN photo: English text, OP06-093, full-art, Koushi Rokushiro credit, no prize stamp; JP set: JP text, same parallel artwork); manual corner-quad overlays visually reviewed before use; two auto-quads rejected on inspection

observations.statement: Two private Lane A bundles exist (EN and JP Perona OP06-093 alternate art), each with an append-only promotion ledger opened at hypothesis, hash-recorded media, and manifests whose digest, hash, task, and tier checks all pass; all media and diagnostics remain outside the repository.
observations.source: optcg-reference validate output, private bundle storage

observations.statement: The EN bundle holds 14 sources (12 blocked with human acquisition tasks, 1 official canonical scan, 1 pixel-verified raw EN seller photo) and is Tier C: one photo from one seller cannot carry a bundle.
The JP bundle registered 3 of 11 seller photos (one via reviewed manual quad: 658 inliers, 0.91px reprojection); steep-tilt, occluded, and cornerless macro inputs were rejected rather than forced.

The JP appearance envelope produced schema-valid observed-appearance proposals with confidences 0.39-0.58; the two-source specular agreement rule correctly returned zero activation for photos lit from different positions.
observations.source: JP bundle appearance envelope + diagnostics sidecar

observations.statement: Both JP fitting attempts were rejected by coded gates: the watermarked-scan albedo triggered the renderer-model-limit diagnostic (aggregate loss 0.361), and the median-of-photos albedo improved to 0.229 but failed the acceptance gate (0 sources at accept threshold, consistency 0.021 below the 0.35 floor). No profile was accepted; no threshold was tuned to force acceptance.
All three registered JP sources scored 0.499 against the 0.50 Tier-B floor; per-source scoring does not aggregate cross-source angle diversity.

inferences.statement: The dominant fit-rejection causes are three inconsistent unknown lighting setups, compression, and albedo contamination; a single-session tilt video or one-lighting multi-angle set is the highest-leverage acquisition for either bundle.
The 0.499-vs-0.50 tier outcome and the zero specular activation on small heterogeneous sets are threshold-calibration questions for the reviewer, not code defects.

known_failures: JP reference fit rejected twice by design gates (model-limit, then acceptance gate) — recorded outcome, not silently retried
Two subagent runs terminated by upstream API overload; the pipeline was completed inline by the lead session
Semantic proposals deferred: SAM 2.1 checkpoint not installed and the only clean art carries a database watermark
Adversarial critic not invoked: no accepted profile exists to disprove
unresolved_questions: Tier-B floor calibration (0.50) vs per-source single-angle penalty on multi-angle bundles
Whether to promote holo-lab into main (operator decision pending; standing instruction forbids it)
Bundle-review publication adapter design (reference publication remains fail-closed)
JP batch-to-listing attribution inferred from operator message order; awaiting operator confirmation

recommended_state_transition.justification: Both bundles remain at hypothesis: exact-variant-verified is a human-only transition and no bundle has reached an eligible tier; no advancement is proposed.
